data.title: Dragonfly Partner: The current bear market is a piece of cake compared to 2022.
data.description: Haseeb, Managing Partner of Dragonfly, wrote on the X platform that the current bear market may be the "easiest" bear market yet. Many people seem to have forgotten what 2022 was like: Luna crashed, then 3AC, then FTX, then Genesis, BlockFi, Axie, NFTs—almost everything teetered on the brink of collapse, like a house of cards. Then, after all that collapsed, banks failed, stablecoins went detached from their pegs, and Gary Gensler attempted to destroy almost every company in the industry. In comparison, the current market situation is "a piece of cake" compared to 2022; although prices have fallen, the fundamentals remain strong.

data.description:

data.title: The WLFI multisignature wallet transferred 166.6 million WLFI to seven new wallets, worth $23.74 million.
data.description: According to The Data Nerd, two hours ago, a multi-signature wallet (0x33C...B78) of the WLFI project transferred 166.6 million WLFI (approximately $23.74 million) to seven new wallets, with each wallet receiving 23.8 million WLFI. Furthermore, all WLFI tokens held by early buyers remain locked.

data.title: Security Alert Issued for Malicious Chrome Extension Disguised as Ethereum Wallet
data.description:
According to PANews, GoPlus has issued a security alert regarding a malicious Chrome extension masquerading as an Ethereum (ETH) wallet. Released on November 12, 2024, this extension is designed to steal user assets by encoding mnemonic phrases into Sui transactions.

Promoted as a simple and secure ETH wallet, the extension contains a backdoor that encodes user mnemonic phrases into Sui addresses. It then broadcasts micro-transactions from a Sui wallet controlled by the attacker, making it highly covert.

As of now, the malicious extension has not been removed from the Chrome Web Store. GoPlus has reported the issue to Chrome and blacklisted the download link.

The extension is named 'Safery: Ethereum Wallet,' and the attacker's email is kifagusertyna@gmail[.]com.

data.title: This week's top mining news highlights: Cango's total Bitcoin holdings surpass 6,500, and its October operational efficiency exceeds 90%.
data.description: Week 44 of 2025 (November 7th - November 13th): 1. According to cloverpool, the average hashrate of the Bitcoin network was 1081 EH/s, with a high of 1186 EH/s and a low of 930 EH/s, a decrease of 0.86% compared to the previous week's average hashrate (1090 EH/s). 2. According to blockchain.com, the average price of Bitcoin was $103,178, with a high of $107,482 and a low of $97,870, a decrease of 3.76% compared to the previous week's average price ($107,206). 2. Notable mining news includes: (1) Cango mined 126.4 BTC last week, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to over 6,500; (2) TeraWulf's Q3 revenue reached $50.6 million, up 87% year-over-year; (3) CleanSpark expanded its convertible bond issuance to $1.15 billion; (4) Bitdeer will raise $400 million through convertible senior notes; (5) Bitfarms released its Q3 financial report: currently holding 1,827 BTC with total liquidity of $814 million; (6) IREN signed a $9.7 billion agreement with Microsoft to provide cloud computing services in Texas. Data partner: NYSE-listed Bitcoin mining company Cango Inc. (CANG).

data.title: El Salvador continues to increase his Bitcoin holdings, now holding 6,380 coins.
data.description: According to data from the Salvadoran Ministry of Finance website, El Salvador continues to add one Bitcoin daily, currently holding 6,380.18 Bitcoins, valued at approximately $630 million. In the past seven days, they have added eight Bitcoins. With the decline in BTC prices, El Salvador's Bitcoin investment is now showing a paper profit of $317 million, returning to levels seen in April and May of this year.

data.title: The US Solana spot ETF saw a net inflow of $1.49 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, on November 13th (Eastern Time), the US Solana spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $1.49 million. The Bitwise Solana spot ETF (BSOL) also saw a single-day net inflow of $1.49 million, with a historical total net inflow of $346 million. The Grayscale Solana spot ETF (GSOL) saw no net inflow, with a historical total net inflow of $24.32 million. As of press time, the Solana spot ETF's total net asset value was $533 million, with a net asset value ratio of 0.64%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $370 million.

data.title: Alliance DAO co-founder: The top of the 4-year crypto cycle is emerging; the US stock market AI bubble will dominate price movements.
data.description: QwQiao, co-founder of Alliance DAO, recently wrote that while macroeconomic factors such as the Federal Reserve's quantitative easing (QE), the rebuilding of the US Treasury's general account (TGA), and interest rate cuts point to a market rally, intuitively, it's all over. QwQiao describes crypto as a self-fulfilling asset class, emphasizing the inevitability of a four-year cycle prophecy, leaving the market at a frustrating crossroads. A long-term optimist, he has been uneasy about the crypto market since mid-September, observing that most savvy traders and long-term investors have turned bearish. Turning to US stocks, QwQiao sees artificial intelligence (AI) as the sole driver of the cycle, far outweighing liquidity indicators and technical signals. He warns that if the AI ​​bubble bursts, the entire market will collapse; conversely, if AI-related stocks continue to rise, bears will be completely wrong. He compares NVIDIA (NVDA) to Bitcoin in the crypto world, pointing out that when AI stocks (especially NVIDIA) rise, funds flow out of crypto and other assets, causing crypto to fall, and vice versa, creating a binary situation of AI stocks vs. everything. In specific sectors, QwQiao is optimistic about the growth momentum of stablecoin startups, believing it far surpasses that of AI startups. This is mainly due to the scarcity of competition in the stablecoin market, while the AI ​​vertical is already crowded with more than 50 players. This year, his US stock investment returns have significantly exceeded those of crypto, and he has avoided high-growth but inefficient AI stocks, instead favoring high-quality companies at reasonable prices.

data.title: Binance to End Galxe (GAL) Deposits via Ethereum Network
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform will discontinue support for deposits of Galxe (GAL) through the Ethereum Network (ERC20) starting from 2025-11-19 03:00 (UTC). After this deadline, any GAL deposits made via the Ethereum Network will not be credited to users' accounts, potentially resulting in asset loss.

Users are advised to utilize other networks supported by Binance for depositing GAL tokens. This change is part of Binance's ongoing efforts to streamline its services and ensure the security of user assets. The announcement emphasizes the importance of adhering to the specified networks to avoid any complications or loss of funds. Users should remain informed about the supported networks to ensure seamless transactions.

This update reflects Binance's commitment to maintaining a secure and efficient platform for its users. The decision to cease support for GAL deposits via the Ethereum Network is a strategic move to enhance the overall user experience and safeguard digital assets. Users are encouraged to stay updated with Binance's announcements for any further changes or updates regarding supported networks and services.

data.title: Asian Markets Experience Broad Decline at Opening
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Asian stock markets opened with significant losses. In Japan, the Nikkei 225 index fell by 666.59 points, marking a 1.30% decrease. South Korea's KOSPI index dropped 114.2 points, a decline of 2.74%. China's A-shares also saw a downturn, with the Shanghai Composite Index down by 0.56%, the Shenzhen Component Index falling 1.14%, and the ChiNext Index decreasing by 1.74%. Meanwhile, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index opened with a 1.52% drop, and the Hang Seng Tech Index fell by 2.22%. These declines reflect a challenging start for Asian markets.

data.title: Trader Loses $500,000 on U.S. Government Shutdown Bet
data.description: According to Foresight News, a trader using the handle haeju.eth reported a significant financial loss after betting on the end date of a U.S. government shutdown. The trader initially accumulated $400,000 in profits but lost $500,000 after wagering that the shutdown would conclude on November 12. However, the rules set by Polymarket, the platform used for the bet, stipulate that the outcome is only considered correct if the U.S. Office of Personnel Management (OPM) website is updated to reflect the end of the shutdown. As the OPM website did not update, the trader's bet was deemed incorrect.

data.title: Altcoin News Today: Significant Gainers Include RESOLV, ALCX, QNT
data.description: Key TakeawaysResolv (RESOLV price) jumps 30.58% on strong bullish momentum and protocol-driven supply tighteningQuant (QNT price) rises 12.86%, supported by institutional interest in UK tokenized deposits infrastructureAlchemix (ALCX price) climbs 18.19%, fueled by DeFi narrative strength and exchange-driven liquidity The Altcoins Gaining Momentum Amid Wider Crypto Market LossesCertain altcoins are posting significant gains today despite overall weakness in the larger crypto market (-2.86%). Even as Bitcoin falls below its key level of $100K, strong DeFi narratives are driving the momentum behind altcoins like RESOLV and ALCX. RESOLV Leads Altcoin Gainers With 33% SurgeRESOLV has managed to extend its rally from the previous week (+47.15% WoW), confirming a technical breakout, driven by steady protocol buybacks, and short-term exchange-driven dynamics.RESOLV cleared its $0.18 resistance, backed by a 7-day RSI of 79 and MACD histogram at +0.01139, signaling extreme bullish momentum. Volume reached 83.2M, and the market now watches whether $0.145—near the 23.6% Fibonacci retracement ($0.1418)—holds as support.Source: DuneA weekly program using 75% of protocol fees to remove tokens from circulation continues to apply upside pressure. Supply reduction at a relatively modest 31.78M circulating supply heightens sensitivity to buying pressure during high-volume periods.  QNT Advances Nearly 12% on Institutional MomentumQNT price updates show QNT rising 11.47% to $91.46, supported by a blend of technical strength and enterprise adoption through the UK’s tokenized deposits initiative.QNT broke above its 50-day EMA ($84.73) and Fibonacci 23.6% retracement ($86.76), while the RSI at 58.33 (neutral) and MACD +1.3 indicate continued bullish momentum. Traders now watch resistance near $98.63. Source: CoinMarketCapDespite extreme fear in markets (index 22/100), QNT benefited from its low correlation to Bitcoin and a narrative centered on real-world financial use cases, attracting capital amid macro uncertainty. Alchemix Surges by 26% as DeFi Narrative StrengthensALCX is up 26.05%, adding to strong 7-day (+39%) gains as traders re-enter DeFi sectors.The v3 upgrade bringing 90% LTV self-repaying loans has pushed 24h trading volume to $95.8M (+49.39%).ALCX moved above its 200-day EMA ($9.72) and $14.90 resistance, with RSI at 69.37 and MACD +0.57 highlighting rising momentum. Traders monitor $14.90 as a key level for trend continuation.Drops in Bitcoin dominance (59.42%) and rotation into higher-beta assets contributed to ALCX’s advance, though its $35.8M market cap underscores sensitivity to shifts in market confidence. Outlook: Can Momentum for These Altcoins Hold?While bullish momentum, protocol catalysts, and institutional developments supported today’s altcoin gains, sentiment remains low-confidence across the broader market.Key levels to watch:RESOLV: $0.145 support after margin delistingQNT: $98.63 resistance amid macro volatilityALCX: $14.90 resistance as RSI approaches overheated territory

data.title: The US Hedera spot ETF saw a net inflow of $5.37 million in a single day; the Litecoin spot ETF saw a net inflow of $698,000 in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, on November 13th (Eastern Time), the Canary HBAR ETF (HBR) saw a net inflow of $5.37 million. As of press time, the Canary HBAR ETF's total net asset value was $68.8 million, with an HBAR net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of HBAR's total market capitalization) of 0.91%. The Canary Litecoin ETF (LTCC) saw a net inflow of $698,000. As of press time, the Canary Litecoin ETF's total net asset value was $6.05 million, with an LTC net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of LTC's total market capitalization) of 0.05%.
